BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 Core Variants

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned U06 engine typically costs £1,800–£9,500 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B38 1.5 petrol sits at £1,800–£3,500, while the 230e PHEV can reach £9,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on engine and specialist location. The U06's strong retained values mean replacement is almost always viable.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48A20P 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol and the B38A15P 1.5-litre three-cylinder petrol are the most reliable options — both are proven, well-understood designs with fewer major failure modes than the PHEV variants. The B47 diesel is also reliable, though the EGR cooler is a known weak point [PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Page].

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system leaks on the B38 and B48 petrol engines are the most common issue — water pump and hose replacement is routine maintenance. The B47 diesel can suffer from EGR cooler failure. The PHEV variants may experience battery degradation over time. The 48V mild-hybrid system on the 220i can develop issues.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, the U06 is generally reliable — it uses BMW's proven B-family engines that have been in production for several years. The petrol engines are particularly robust. The PHEV variants are more complex but early reliability is promising. The U06's FAAR platform is shared with the F40 1 Series, which has a good reliability record.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is a hard avoid, but the PHEV variants require careful consideration — the battery and electric motor add significant complexity, and battery degradation is a long-term risk. If you're buying used, get a battery health report before committing. The 48V MHEV system on the 220i also adds complexity versus the non-MHEV 218i.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my U06 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss is the key warning sign on B38 and B48 engines — investigate any drop in coolant level immediately. On B47 diesels, reduced performance or warning lights may indicate EGR cooler failure. On PHEVs, reduced electric range is the main sign of battery degradation. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £15,000 U06, a £2,800–£6,000 B47 recon is 19–40% — safe. Even on a lower-value £12,000 example, the same recon is 23–50% — well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold [BMW-VERIFIED]. The U06's strong retained values mean engine replacement is almost always viable.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B38, B48 and B47 engines can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing — cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity on the petrols, while EGR cooler care is important on the diesels. The PHEV battery is rated for approximately 1,500–2,000 charge cycles before significant degradation.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the U06 and the F45 2 Series Active Tourer?

acceptedAnswer.text: The U06 (2021–present) is the second-generation Active Tourer, built on BMW's FAAR platform with updated B-family engines and MHEV/PHEV technology. The F45 (2014–2021) was the first generation, built on the UKL platform with older N-family engines. The U06 is larger, more refined, and has more advanced engine technology.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the engine block. Tell us your variant (216i, 218i, 220i, 223i, 218d, 220d, 223d, 225e, or 230e) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the U06 Active Tourer — spanning B38 three-cylinder petrols, B48 four-cylinder petrols, B47 four-cylinder diesels, and plug-in hybrids with up to 326 bhp.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are [PROVISIONAL] pending dedicated Engine Pages. The B38 three-cylinder petrol is a proven, reliable engine — it's been in production since 2013 and powers numerous BMW and MINI models. The B48 four-cylinder is similarly well-regarded. The B47 diesel is significantly more reliable than the older N47 it replaced — timing chain issues are far less frequent. The PHEV variants (225e, 230e) add battery and electric motor complexity — battery degradation is the primary long-term concern.

overview.h2: BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 — Overview
overview.intro: The U06 is the second generation of the BMW 2 Series Active Tourer, unveiled in October 2021 as the successor to the F45. Built on BMW's FAAR (Frontantriebsarchitektur) platform — shared with the latest 1 Series and 2 Series Gran Coupé — the compact MPV grew slightly in size (now 4,386 mm long, 1,824 mm wide) while retaining the 2,670 mm wheelbase. The engine line-up is all-turbocharged, featuring B38 three-cylinder petrols, B48 four-cylinder petrols, B47 four-cylinder diesels, and two plug-in hybrids (225e xDrive and 230e xDrive) with up to 87 km (54 miles) of electric range. A significant July 2024 update added the 220d diesel to the range.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW FAAR (Frontantriebsarchitektur) • Predecessor: F45 2 Series Active Tourer • Successor: None (current generation) • Sibling body styles in this generation: None (2 Series Gran Tourer discontinued).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The U06 2 Series Active Tourer generated 147+ real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 218d diesel and 220i MHEV petrol as the model reaches the used market.

items.quote: The 2.0-litre B48 is a gem — smooth, responsive, and with mild-hybrid assistance for seamless stop-start. The 223i offers the best blend of performance and refinement in the U06 range, with 218 bhp and 360 Nm of torque.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want proper performance without the PHEV's complexity.

items.quote: The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47 — timing chain issues are far less frequent, and the engine is smoother and more refined. The 220d, added in July 2024, offers the best blend of performance and economy in the diesel range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel economy without the N47's timing chain anxiety.

items.quote: The plug-in hybrids are the most complex powertrains in the U06 range — a 1.5-litre turbo engine, an electric motor, and a 14.2 kWh battery pack. More components mean more potential failure points, and battery replacement is a significant cost.
items.whoItsFor: company car drivers and buyers who prioritise electric range over mechanical simplicity.

items.quote: The most powerful U06 engine — and the most expensive to replace. The B38 engine, electric motor, and battery pack combine for a replacement cost that could exceed £9,500. Battery degradation is the primary long-term concern.
items.whoItsFor: buyers with warranty cover who want 326 bhp and low company car tax.

items.quote: The entry-level petrol is the smart buy for most UK buyers — 136 bhp is more than adequate for family duties, the three-cylinder is characterful and efficient, and the engine is simpler than the PHEV or 2.0-litre options. Lower running costs and proven B38 architecture make this the value champion.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who prioritise running costs and don't need extra power.

items.quote: The 220i is often overlooked because it's 'just' a three-cylinder — but with 48V mild-hybrid assistance, it produces 170 bhp and 280 Nm of torque, making it quicker than the 218i and more efficient than the 223i. The electric boost fills in the turbo lag and makes city driving a breeze.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the best balance of performance, efficiency, and simplicity.

ownershipEconomics.h2: BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 — Ownership Economics

rows.ownershipVerdict: Solid Choice — MHEV adds complexity but proven.
Safe Bet — the most refined petrol.
Solid Choice — significantly improved over N47.
Watch — battery health is key; warranty essential.

economicsRule.title: The U06 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£6,000 B47 recon against a £12,000–£30,000 Active Tourer sits at 9–50% of the car's value【EM-VERIFIED】. Even on a lower-value example, that's well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold — replacement is almost always viable. The U06's strong retained values mean engine replacement is a repair decision, not a financial one. For PHEV cars, the battery is the bigger long-term consideration.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U06 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B38 218i — simple three-cylinder, fewer failure modes, cheap parts.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U06 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 230e xDrive PHEV — battery and electric motor add significant long-term cost risk.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U06 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B47 218d — excellent fuel economy, proven B47 reliability, and strong residual values.

problems.title: B38 / B48 Cooling System Leaks
problems.rootCause: The B-family engines (B38, B48) are generally reliable, but they can suffer from coolant leaks from the water pump and plastic cooling system components — a known weakness on BMW's modular engine family.

tieredCosts.work: Full engine replacement if overheating caused damage

problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected annually and replace the water pump as preventative maintenance at 60,000 miles — a £400–£800 specialist job is trivial against the £3,500+ engine replacement that follows an ignored coolant leak.

cta.label: Compare B38/B48 engine prices for your U06 →

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler Failure
problems.rootCause: The B47's EGR cooler can develop internal leaks, allowing coolant to enter the exhaust system. This can lead to reduced performance and, in extreme cases, engine damage. BMW issued recalls for this issue on some models.

tieredCosts.work: Full engine replacement if coolant contamination caused damage

problems.recommendation: Check your B47 U06's VIN against any outstanding EGR cooler recalls — if affected, the repair is free. If not, have the EGR cooler inspected at 60,000 miles — a £500–£1,000 replacement is cheap insurance against a £4,000+ engine failure.

cta.label: Compare B47 diesel engine prices for your U06 →

problems.title: U06 PHEV Battery Degradation & Electric Motor Issues
problems.rootCause: The 14.2 kWh lithium-ion battery pack will naturally degrade over time, reducing electric-only range. The electric motor adds complexity to the powertrain.
problems.recommendation: Have the PHEV battery health checked annually — a £300–£800 inspection is cheap insurance against a £6,500+ battery replacement. If you're buying a used U06 PHEV, get a battery health report before committing.

problems.title: B38 48V Mild-Hybrid System Issues
problems.rootCause: The 48V mild-hybrid system adds complexity — the 48V battery, electric motor, and associated electronics can develop issues. The 48V battery typically has a lifespan of 5–8 years.
problems.recommendation: Have the 48V system inspected during annual servicing — a £500–£1,000 check is cheap insurance against a £2,800+ system replacement. The mild-hybrid system is a major selling point — protect it.

replacementCosts.h2: U06 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the EM_Proprietary_Data baseline figures — U06 Active Tourer shares the FAAR platform with the F40 1 Series, with similar engine-bay access, so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are U06 2 Series Active Tourer only. 220i, 223i and 223d feature 48V mild-hybrid technology. 225e xDrive (245 bhp) and 230e xDrive (326 bhp) are plug-in hybrids with 14.2 kWh battery and up to 87 km electric range. 220d was added to the range in July 2024.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 2 Series Active Tourer U06 engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.question: What is the difference between the U06 and the F45 2 Series Active Tourer?
items.answer: The U06 (2021–present) is the second-generation 2 Series Active Tourer, built on BMW's FAAR platform with updated B-family engines and mild-hybrid/PHEV technology. The F45 (2014–2021) was the first generation, built on the UKL platform with the older N-family engines (N13, N47). The U06 is larger, more refined, and has more advanced engine technology. The 2 Series Gran Tourer (F46, 7-seater) was discontinued and not replaced.
